This is one of the most precise diagnoses I've seen of this trend. The media and content people mostly consume is the clearest diagnostic mirror of their actual condition.   This is even more honest than a country's GDP figures, more revealing than official speeches, and more predictive than any poll, because it tells you what they are starved for, what they can no longer afford to feel, and what part of their humanity they have had to numb or outsource just to keep functioning.   When a society's dominant entertainment becomes anti-intellectual humor, it's not primarily because the people have become dumber.   It’s simply that the environment has made sustained attention, delayed gratification, and intellectual effort seem like things they cannot afford. The mind under chronic threat defaults to whatever offers the fastest exit from its own reality.   This is why surface-level moralizing about "olodo culture" misses the point entirely. You cannot lecture a drowning man on the importance of swimming technique. First, you have to change the water level.   The content we reward with attention and virality is never neutral. It is always a map of our collective needs. Right now, that map is showing exhaustion and a desperate search for momentary lightness in a country that has made sustained hope feel expensive.   The only real exit right now is the slow, painful work of rebuilding conditions where intelligence and depth offer more relief and compensation than folly content. But until then, the market will keep giving the people exactly what their current reality demands.

🎮 GGDUEL | Alleged User Database Published  A threat actor has released what is claimed to be the full database of GGDUEL, a blockchain-based skill gaming platform.  * According to the forum post, the alleged compromised data includes:   * Usernames   * Email addresses   * Dates of birth   * Wallet addresses   * Linked gaming accounts   * Profile metadata   * Real names   * Postal addresses   * Phone numbers   * IBAN information  * The actor claims the data originated from a June 2026 breach and has published a downloadable CSV dataset along with sample records.  * If authentic, the exposure combines personally identifiable information (PII) with blockchain wallet data, increasing the risk of targeted phishing, account takeover attempts, identity theft, and cryptocurrency-focused scams.  * At the time of publication, there is no independent verification confirming the authenticity of the dataset or the claimed breach.  Analyst Note: Datasets linking real-world identities with cryptocurrency wallets are particularly valuable to threat actors. Affected users should remain alert for phishing campaigns, verify wallet interactions carefully, rotate reused credentials, and enable MFA wherever possible.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Crypto

Fear arises where there is a second.  As the Brihadaranyaka Upanishad says, “Where there is another, there is fear.”  When what is feared is recognised as being of the same nature as oneself, fear ceases to dominate experience. It is no longer experienced as an existential threat to a separate self.  What remains is intelligent responsiveness.  If a cobra appears, there is no need to fear it in the psychological sense. But there is every reason to avoid it. The organism steps back, just as it withdraws a hand from a hot stove.  The cobra and the one avoiding it are expressions of the same reality. Yet within the regional world of form, discrimination remains essential. To embrace the cobra because “all is One” is simply confusion. Death, too, is of the same nature as oneself—but that does not make recklessness wisdom.  Nonduality does not abolish discrimination. It abolishes the illusion of separation while leaving practical intelligence entirely intact.

Palo Alto Networks Unit 42は、中国語話者のAPTグループ「CL-STA-1062」が、東南アジアの政府機関や重要インフラを狙う攻撃で、独自バックドア「TinyRCT」を使用していると報告した。  Unit 42によると、CL-STA-1062は2022年3月以降、東アジアの戦略分野を継続的に標的にしており、2025年には東南アジアのエネルギーや政府部門の国有企業を狙った。同グループは、台湾のWeb基盤を狙ったUAT-7237と重複する特徴を持つという。  2025年9月の攻撃では、政府機関へ侵入し、WebシェルでMS SQLサーバーからデータを窃取した。同じ国の別の政府機関へのネットワーク偵察も確認され、10月から12月には少なくとも10組織の侵害を検知した。  攻撃ではASPX Webシェルを足場にし、SoftEther VPN、Mimikatz、VNTなどを利用する。これらはVMware実行ファイルやXDRエージェントに偽装されていた。  TinyRCTは.NET製RATで、任意コマンド実行、ファイル送受信、画面取得、痕跡削除に対応する。HTTPでC2と通信し、AES-128-CBCでデータを暗号化する。 thehackernews.com/2026/06/ch…

sample_tweets.text: „Die Streitkräfte der Ukraine haben seit Beginn des Krieges 2,4 Millionen Soldaten verloren“ – Daten von Hackerangriffen. Die Ukraine hat in vier Jahren des Krieges 2,4 Millionen Soldaten verloren. Allein in den letzten sechs Monaten werden die Verluste auf 400.000 Tote geschätzt.  - Diese Informationen wurden von den ukrainischen Hackern PalachPro und der Gruppe NoName057 (16) aus gehackten Datenbanken des Generalstabs der Streitkräfte der Ukraine, des Territorialverteidigungskommandos, ukrainischer medizinischer Einrichtungen und Leichenhallen beschafft, berichtet ein Korrespondent von „PolitNavigator“.  - Es wird festgestellt, dass bis August 2025 die Zahl der getöteten Soldaten der Streitkräfte der Ukraine 1,7 Millionen erreichte, bis Dezember die Zahl über 2 Millionen überschritt. In den sechs Monaten des Jahres 2026 verloren die Streitkräfte der Ukraine genauso viele wie im gesamten Jahr 2023 – etwa 400.000 Menschen.  - Die größten Verluste gab es in den Richtungen Pokrowsk, Konstantinowsk, Liman, Saporischschje und Kupjansk – auf jeder von ihnen starben im Durchschnitt 500 Soldaten der Streitkräfte der Ukraine pro Tag. Zu den führenden Brigaden gehören die 72. und 110. motorisierte Schützenbrigade, die Luftlandetruppen und die Territorialverteidigungskräfte.  - Bemerkenswert ist, dass der Tod ausländischer Söldner als Unfälle verschleiert wird. Nach Angaben der Quelle starben etwa 5.000 Ausländer in den Reihen der Streitkräfte der Ukraine. Es wird festgestellt, dass es sich bei den meisten jungen Männern aus Brasilien und Argentinien um 20- bis 23-Jährige handelt.  - Dennoch wird die Verstärkung der Einheiten der Streitkräfte durch ausländische Spezialisten regelmäßig fortgesetzt, und damit die Verlustzahlen weiter steigen.  t.me/boris_rozhin/216032

❗️「ウクライナの軍隊は戦争開始以来240万人の兵士を失った」 - ハッカーによるデータ漏洩。 ウクライナは4年間の戦争で240万人の兵士を失いました。この半年間だけで40万人以上の死者が出ています。  🔴このような情報は、ウクライナのハッカー集団「PalachPro」と「NoName057」(16)によって、ウクライナの国防省、国家安全保障局、医療機関、死体安置所のデータベースから入手されました。「ポリトナビゲーター」の特派員が報告しています。   🔴報告によると、2025年8月までにウクライナの軍隊で死亡した人数は170万人に達したが、12月までにその数字は200万人を超えた。2026年の6か月間に、ウクライナの軍隊は2023年全体と同じくらいの人数を失った - 約40万人。  🔴最も多くの損失がポクロフスク、コンスタンチノフスク、リマンスク、ザポリジア、クピアンスクの各戦線で発生した - それぞれの戦線で平均500人のウクライナの兵士が毎日死亡しています。 旅団の中で主導的な役割を果たしているのは第72旅団、第110旅団、特殊部隊、テロ防衛部隊などです。   🔴興味深いことに、外国人傭兵の死は事故として処理されている。情報筋によれば、約5,000人の外国人がウクライナの軍隊で死亡した。 指摘されているのは、ほとんどが20～23歳のブラジルやアルゼンチンから来た若者たちであることです。   🔴とはいえ、外国人専門家による武装集団の増援は定期的に続いており、そのため、損失の数字は増え続けている。  🔴また、以下の記事もお読みください: ウクライナのドローン操縦士の指揮官:「今年はこれまでの全年と同じくらいの人員を失った」。

ウクライナは、2022年にロシアがウクライナで作戦を開始して以来、約240万人の兵士を失いました。  ロシアのハッカー集団PalachProとNoName057(16)グループが、ウクライナ参謀本部とウクライナの領土募集センター（TCCs）のデータベースに侵入しました。  彼らはまた、ウクライナの医療機関と検死所もハッキングしました。  漏洩したデータによると、損失は2025年8月時点で170万人に達し、12月には200万人を超えました。2026年の最初の6か月だけで、キエフに約40万人の人的損失を与えたとされ、これは2023年全体の総損失に相当します。最も被害の大きいのは、Krasnoarmeysk（Pokrovsk）、Konstantinovka、Lyman、Zaporozhye、Kupyansk方面で、各方面で1日あたり平均約500人のAFU損失が発生しています。  Mashはまた、外国傭兵の死亡が戦闘損失として記録されなくなったと報じており、代わりに事故として記録されているそうです。漏洩データによると、AFUに所属する約5,000人の外国人戦闘員が死亡しており、その数はTCCsが外国人（多くは20〜23歳のアルゼンチン人とブラジル人）を引き続き募集しているため増加しているとされています。  PalachProとNoName057(16)は、Mashが以前報じた通り、ウクライナとEUの約50,000台の監視カメラにAI支援の顔認識技術を展開したのと同じグループです。

ロシアのハッカー集団PalachProとNoName057(16)グループが、ウクライナ参謀本部とウクライナの領土募集センター（TCCs）のデータベースに侵入して突き止めたようで、何とウクライナ側の兵士の死者数が約240万人とのこと（本当か？）  英政府通信本部（GCHQ）という組織が発表したロシア側死者が50万人というプロパガンダとは全く違う手法たるハッキングで突き止めたのは面白い。 もちろん信じる信じないは貴方次第。だが以前に書いたが自軍の死者数が本当に分かっているのは当事者たるロシアでありウクライナである。ウクライナのデータベースからの情報なので無視はできない。もちろんロシア側のプロパガンダかもしれないが、この件を主要メディアで報じているかどうか。

According to the post, the actor alleges access to live surveillance camera feeds covering various areas of the museum, including entrances, courtyards, exhibition halls, and visitor areas. The claim is accompanied by screenshots purportedly showing the CCTV environment.  If authentic, unauthorized access to surveillance systems could present privacy and physical security concerns by exposing live monitoring capabilities and potentially revealing information about facility operations and visitors.  At the time of writing, the authenticity of the claim, the extent of any alleged access, and whether the museum’s CCTV infrastructure was compromised have not been independently verified. No official confirmation regarding the alleged incident has been identified.  #CyberSecurity #France #CCTV #PhysicalSecurity #ThreatIntel #Surveillance #SecurityAlert

Grok bedömer att ukrainska förluster kommer uppgå till tre miljoner någon gång mars/april nästa år.  👇  Baserat på postens uppgifter: cirka 2,4 miljoner totala förluster (döda + sårade) för Ukrainas sida fram till juni 2026. @i  Posten (från Chay Bowes, juni 2025-uppdaterad) bygger på påstådda hacks från ryska grupper (KillNet, PalachPro, NoName057(16) m.fl.) av ukrainska databaser, General Staff, TCC (rekryteringscenter) och sjukhus/morgar.   Nyckeltal: •  Augusti 2025: ~1,7 miljoner. •  December 2025: Över 2 miljoner. •  Januari–juni 2026: ~400 000 (motsvarande hela 2023 års totala förluster enligt posten). •  Högsta förlustzoner: Krasnoarmeysk (Pokrovsk), Konstantinovka, Lyman, Zaporizjzja, Kupyansk – genomsnitt ~500 per dag per axel. •  Övrigt: Utländska legosoldater (ca 5 000 döda, ökande) loggas som olyckor istället för stridsförluster. Rekrytering av utländska (bl.a. argentinska och brasilianska) unga män pågår.  Extrapolering till 3 miljoner totala förluster (döda + sårade): Ytterligare 600 000 behövs från dagens nivå (2,4 miljoner). Senaste rapporterade takt: 400 000 på 6 månader = ~66 700 per månad (eller ~2 200 per dag). @i  •  Tid till mål: ~9 månader vid konstant takt. •  Uppskattad tidpunkt: Mars–april 2027 (från juni 2026).  Detta är en rak linjär extrapolering baserat enbart på postens siffror och senaste halvårsdata – ingen hänsyn till förändringar i stridstempo, mobilisering, vapenleveranser, väder, taktik eller andra faktorer. Om takten ökar (t.ex. intensivare offensiv/defensiv kring Pokrovsk-området) kan det ske tidigare; om den minskar (t.ex. genom vapenvila eller utmattning) senare.  Kvalificering av extrapoleringen •  Totala förluster inkluderar både döda (KIA) och sårade (WIA) + saknade. Klassiska militära förhållanden är ofta 1:3 (döda:sårade), men här blandas det och inkluderar MIA/desertörer i vissa rapporter. •  Posten nämner att förlusterna accelererat 2026, med 400k på ett halvår – det är den starkaste basen för kort sikt. •  Långsiktigt är linjär modell osäker: Ukrainas befolkning/mobiliseringspotential är begränsad (~20–25 miljoner pre-war, stor emigration + demografiska problem), så takten kan inte hållas obegränsat utan massiv extern rekrytering eller förändrad doktrin.  Sammanfattningsvis Mars–april 2027 vid nuvarande takt. Detta är ren matematik utifrån de presenterade siffrorna, som du bad om att använda direkt. Verkligheten på slagfältet varierar snabbt.

⚡️Mash reports that Ukraine has lost 2.4 million soldiers in the four years of the SMO, more than 400,000 of them in 2026. These figures were obtained by our hackers from the databases of the General Staff of the Ukrainian Armed Forces, TCC centers, Ukrainian medical organizations and morgues.   By August 2025, the number of eliminated soldiers reached 1.7 million — by December, the figure exceeded 2 million. In the first six months of 2026, the AFU lost as many soldiers as they did in all of 2023 — approximately 400,000. The highest number of deaths occurred on the Pokrovsk, Konstantinovka, Liman, Zaporozhye, and Kupyansk fronts — each of them averaging 500 AFU militants killed per day. The most deaths occurred in the 72nd and 110th Motor Rifle Brigades, airborne assault brigades, and territorial defense forces.  Deaths of foreign mercenaries are no longer recorded among the casualties — they are all attributed to accidents. According to our information, the number of foreigners killed in the Ukrainian Armed Forces is close to five thousand. The figure continues to rise, as the TCC regularly replenishes the ranks of militants with foreigners — 5-6 of whom are recruited by each TCC center in Kiev alone, including many young men (20-23 years of age on average) from Argentina and Brazil.   The Ukrainian databases were hacked by PalachPro hackers and the NoName057 group (16) — these are the same guys who used AI to hack into 50,000 surveillance cameras for surveillance in the Ukraine and EU. Governments have spent TRILLIONS of taxpayer dollars on “green” energy projects, and while growth in renewables has been enormous (particularly solar), it has barely made a dent in the global energy mix (note, these figures are based on the substitution method for fair cross-comparison, so it slightly lowers fossil fuels) because coal, oil, and natural gas consumption continue to increase year after year.  If eco nuts like you actually wanted to “decarbonize” the economy, then a deregulation of and transition to nuclear fission would achieve that far faster.

"Climate change causes earthquakes" is the kind of headline that destroys public trust in science.  Earthquakes are driven by plate tectonics and faults—not atmospheric CO₂. Yes, changes in ice loads or groundwater can, in some places, subtly influence stresses on existing faults, but that's a world away from claiming "massive climate-induced earthquakes are brewing beneath our biggest cities.  Stop turning every natural disaster into climate clickbait.  Sensationalism isn't science. sciencefocus.com/planet-eart…

‼️ An online terrorist group called "Eterssa," linked to the 764 network and the Ukrainian terror group MKY, and apparently run by Russian actors, has had most of its members arrested following an international investigation.  Tracked since January, the group was responsible for school shooting hoaxes and swattings in Texas, claimed credit for vehicle arsons in the US, Germany, and Italy, and later took responsibility for acts including a recent mass shooting in San Diego while flooding social media with propaganda and bombing threats.  The author of the post (Credit: @bx_on_x) became a target after reporting on MKY and 764, receiving threats of assassination and having their house bombed. The group's 17-year-old leader, known as "Flester," was arrested in Dagestan, Russia, where the FSB described him as the founder and administrator of one of the largest international online communities tied to terrorist organizations and released footage of the arrest, one of several arrests reportedly made in recent months.

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Al Barid Bank Data Breach: 1.9 Million Customer SMS Records Exposed on Dark Web  A significant data breach involving Morocco’s Al Barid Bank has been reported, with approximately 1.98 million database entries exposed for sale on dark web forums. The leaked dataset consists primarily of SMS notification records sent to bank customers, containing sensitive personal and financial information.  According to the threat intelligence report dated June 26, 2026, the exposed data fields include: - Phone numbers (Moroccan format +212) - SMS content detailing transaction types (e.g., transfers, check payments, card availability) - Transaction amounts in Moroccan Dirhams (MAD) - Account balances - Partially masked account numbers and card numbers - Timestamps for message queuing, sending, and delivery status - Unique message IDs  Samples from the leak reveal detailed transaction notifications, such as "VIREMENT EMIS VERS COMPTE CHEQUE" (Transfer issued to checking account) with specific amounts and resulting balances. Other samples indicate notifications regarding new card availability at various branches, including those in Rabat, Dakhla, and Had Rharia. Some messages reference military or royal guard branches (e.g., "RABAT ETAT MAJOR FAR", "RABAT LA GARDE ROYALE"), suggesting the presence of high-profile or government-linked accounts within the dataset.  Al Barid Bank is a major financial institution in Morocco, operating under the supervision of the Office des Changes. It serves millions of customers nationwide, particularly in rural areas, and plays a critical role in the country's financial inclusion strategy. The exposure of nearly 2 million customer records poses a severe risk of identity theft, social engineering attacks, and fraud, given the direct link between phone numbers, account balances, and transaction histories.

🚨 A threat actor known as Kazu has posted a listing on an underground forum claiming to have exfiltrated 1.73 TB of data from ConsultorioMovil, a telemedicine and healthcare system provider. The post, dated June 24, 2026, alleges that the breach compromises sensitive medical records and internal operational data associated with the platform.  ConsultorioMovil is a digital health solution designed to facilitate remote patient consultations, electronic health record management, and virtual care delivery. While specific details regarding the company's size or user base are not explicitly detailed in the forum post, telemedicine platforms typically handle highly sensitive Protected Health Information (PHI), making them high-value targets for cybercriminals seeking to exploit medical data on the dark web.  The actor claims the stolen dataset includes: - Patient medical histories and diagnostic reports - Personal identifiable information (PII) of users and healthcare providers - Internal system configurations and administrative credentials - Proprietary software code or database schemas  If authentic, the exposure of such a large volume of healthcare data could lead to significant regulatory penalties under privacy laws like HIPAA or GDPR, depending on the jurisdiction of the affected patients. Additionally, the leak could enable identity theft, insurance fraud, and targeted phishing campaigns against both patients and medical staff.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by security researchers or confirmed by ConsultorioMovil.

🚨 Alleged Leak of 19 Million Australian Telephone Records on Darkweb Forum  A post on an underground forum claims the exposure of a database containing 19 million telephone records belonging to individuals in Australia. The listing, authored by a user identified as 'eCh3l0n' and dated June 24, 2026, alleges that the dataset includes comprehensive contact information for a significant portion of the country's population.  The source material does not specify which telecommunications provider or government agency was targeted, nor does it detail the method used to acquire the data. However, the sheer volume of records suggests a major breach of a central registry or a large-scale aggregation of data from multiple sources. If authentic, such a leak could facilitate widespread phishing campaigns, identity theft, and social engineering attacks against Australian residents.  Australia has seen increased scrutiny regarding data privacy following several high-profile breaches in recent years involving major retailers and healthcare providers. A dataset of this magnitude would represent one of the largest exposures of personal contact information in the nation's history if verified.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, and no official statements have been released by Australian authorities or telecommunications companies confirming the incident.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of Secret PLA Military Documents on Darkweb  A post on an underground forum alleges that a threat actor is offering secret military documents belonging to the People's Liberation Army (PLA) for sale. The listing, published by a user named 'mosad' on June 22, 2026, claims to possess classified materials from China's armed forces.  According to the thread title and description, the exposed data consists of: *   Secret PLA Military Documents  The People's Liberation Army is the unified armed forces of the People's Republic of China and one of the largest standing militaries in the world. If authentic, the exposure of such documents could provide adversaries with insights into Chinese military strategy, capabilities, or internal operations, posing significant national security implications.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, and no samples of the documents have been publicly released for analysis.

🚨 Alleged Robinhood Breach Exposes 14.5 Million User Records on Darkweb    A post on an underground forum listing claims that a database containing the personal information of 14.5 million Robinhood users has been leaked. The actor, operating under the handle 'DuckDB', posted the announcement on June 24, 2026, alleging the exposure of sensitive user data from the popular commission-free trading platform.  Robinhood is a major financial technology company based in the United States, known for its mobile-first approach to investing and cryptocurrency trading. As one of the largest retail brokerage firms in the US, it serves millions of active customers, making any potential breach of its user base a significant security incident with substantial regulatory and reputational implications.  The forum post does not provide specific details regarding the types of data exposed, such as whether it includes passwords, social security numbers, or financial account balances. Nor does it specify the method used to obtain the data or offer proof samples within the initial announcement.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by Robinhood or third-party security researchers. No official statement from the company regarding this specific incident has been released.  #DataBreach #Robinhood #FinTech #DarkWeb #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #USFinance

✈️ Alaska Airlines Account Checker Advertised on Dark Web  A threat actor is advertising an "Alaska

sample_tweets.text: We’ve all seen this meme… and there’s more truth to it than most people realize. 😄  Every cybersecurity career starts somewhere, but growth comes from curiosity, continuous learning, and experience, not just job titles.  Level 1: Learns from mistakes. Level 2: Searches for answers. Level 3: Automates everything. Level 4: Designs secure architectures. Level 5: Thinks about business risk, resilience, and strategy.  The best cybersecurity professionals never stop learning. Even CISOs still Google things, just with a bigger budget and more responsibilities.  Which level are you at today, and what would you add to this list? 👇  #DDW #CyberSecurity #InfoSec #Career

✈️ Alaska Airlines Account Checker Advertised on Dark Web  A threat actor is advertising an "Alaska Airlines Checker v2.0" on a cybercrime forum, claiming the tool validates Alaska Airlines customer account credentials.  * According to the advertisement, the checker supports:   * Bulk credential validation ("combo" checking)   * Full account capture   * CAPTCHA-solving integration   * High-threaded processing   * Filtering of accounts with 2FA enabled  * The screenshot suggests the tool extracts extensive account information from valid logins, including:   * Passenger name   * Date of birth   * Mileage Plan number   * Mileage balance   * Trip history   * Wallet information   * Stored phone numbers   * Saved payment card details (partially masked)  * Tools like these are commonly used with credential stuffing attacks that rely on previously leaked username/password combinations rather than exploiting a vulnerability in the airline itself.  * Daily Dark Web has not independently verified the functionality or effectiveness of the advertised tool.  Analyst Note: Airline loyalty accounts are increasingly targeted because they may contain valuable reward miles, personal information, and stored payment methods. Users should enable MFA where available, use unique passwords, and monitor their loyalty accounts for unauthorized activity.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #AlaskaAirlines

🇫🇷 Influentia.fr | Alleged 49M+ Record Dataset Advertised  A threat actor is advertising an alleged SQL database associated with Influentia.fr, a French influencer marketing platform.  * The post claims the dataset contains approximately 49 million structured records (~2.2 GB SQL database).  * According to the advertisement, the alleged dataset includes:   * Social media profile identifiers and account references   * Engagement metrics (followers, following, posts)   * Biography and profile metadata   * Location and language information   * Platform classification and activity indicators   * User profile settings and associated attributes  * Based on the visible sample, the data appears to consist primarily of influencer profile and analytics information rather than highly sensitive financial or authentication records.  * At the time of publication, there is no independent verification that the dataset is authentic or originated from Influentia.fr.  Analyst Note: Large-scale marketing and analytics datasets can enable OSINT enrichment, targeted phishing, impersonation campaigns, and social engineering by correlating public profile information with additional intelligence sources. Organizations should validate the claim, review access logs, investigate potential database exposure, and assess whether any non-public customer data may have been affected.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France
Riddler vs ChatGPT 😂

🇲🇾 MLIT.com.my & JAG Group | Alleged Full Corporate Network Leak  A threat actor claims to be offering a full dump allegedly linked to MLIT.com.my and JAG Group.  * According to the forum post, the exposed data allegedly includes:   * Corporate email accounts (@jaggroup.com)   * Active Directory domain logins and claimed plaintext passwords   * Microsoft Dynamics GP databases   * Financial records, including P&L statements, revenue reports, ledgers, and clawback data   * SQL Server connection information and IM.mdb databases   * Internal network directory structures and file shares   * Project management files, purchasing records, and sales import logs   * Software license keys and system configuration data   * Compressed internal archives and backup files  * If authentic, this would represent a significant compromise affecting both corporate operations and financial systems, with potential exposure of credentials, business intelligence, and internal infrastructure.  * At the time of publication, these claims have not been independently verified.  Analyst Note: Alleged leaks containing Active Directory credentials, ERP databases, and financial documentation should be treated as high priority. Organizations should immediately investigate the claims, rotate privileged credentials, review privileged account activity, validate backup integrity, and hunt for signs of lateral movement or unauthorized access.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Malaysia

🌐 shopridleys.com | Alleged Database Sale  A threat actor is advertising the alleged sale of a database associated with shopridleys.com on a cybercrime forum.  The post includes:   * A sample of the purported database   * Contact information directing interested buyers to Telegram   * No claimed record count or dataset size in the advertisement  * Based on the visible sample, the dataset appears to contain customer-related records, though the full scope of exposed information has not been disclosed.  * At the time of writing, there is no independent verification confirming the authenticity of the dataset or whether it originated from shopridleys.com.  Analyst Note: Even when record counts are not disclosed, threat actors frequently publish samples to attract buyers. Organizations should investigate the claim, review authentication logs, monitor for unauthorized access, rotate potentially exposed credentials, and notify affected users if a compromise is confirmed.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #DataLeak

🇱🇰 Sri Lanka | Olee AI | Alleged Data Breach  A threat actor has claimed to have breached Sri Lanka-based Olee AI and published what appears to be sample internal data.  * According to the post, the exposed information includes:   * User database records   * 27 employee/admin accounts   * Email addresses   * UUIDs   * Employee profile information   * Position and supervisor details   * Account status information  * The actor claims the incident occurred several weeks before the public post and released sample records to support the claim.  * At the time of writing, there is no independent verification confirming the authenticity of the leaked data or the scope of any compromise.  Analyst Note: Even relatively small internal employee datasets can facilitate targeted phishing, credential stuffing, business email compromise (BEC), and social engineering campaigns. Organizations should validate the claims, rotate exposed credentials, review privileged accounts, and monitor for suspicious authentication activity.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #SriLanka

🇷🇺 Russia | Bauman Moscow State Technical University | Alleged Military & Intelligence Training Archives Leaked  A threat actor has published what they claim are internal archives from Bauman Moscow State Technical University (BMSTU), one of Russia's leading technical universities.  * According to the post, the archive contains:   * 1,932 internal documents   * Student records allegedly linked to military and intelligence training programs   * Training course materials   * Administrative documents   * University publications and presentations  * The post further claims the archive includes:   * Student assignments to military or intelligence units   * Personal information of students participating in security-related programs   * Documents related to military education and recruitment   * Materials characterized by the publisher as supporting Russia's war effort  * At the time of writing, there is no independent verification that the leaked documents are authentic or that the claims made by the publisher are accurate.  Analyst Note: Leaks involving academic institutions with defense or government affiliations may contain a mixture of authentic documents, outdated material, or disinformation. Any intelligence derived from such disclosures should be independently validated before operational or strategic use.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Russia

sample_tweets.text: Chat, we are cooking.  Previously on Dragon Ball Z, someone DM'd me a spoopy GitHub they found. They asked if it was malware. It was malware.  The GitHub contained HEAVILY obfuscated Lua. The malware payload is using Prometheus Obfuscator.  Upon review, it was determined this malware is SmartLoader. SmartLoader is a malware campaign heavily associated with Rhadamanthys Stealer and StealC Stealer.  SmartLoader is relatively new and is being tracked by AhnLabs, TrendMicro, Hexastrike, McAfee, and the GitHub security team. It first emerged around March, 2024.  SmartLoader is pretty sophisticated. It is multi-staged, uses Polygon Smart Contracts for C2 information retrieval, and despite being Lua, it is also makes usage of NTDLL makes low-level WINAPI function invocations. One interesting attribute also is it programmatically inflates or deflates its file size for pseudo-polymorphism. This is extremely cool.  I mention this, and the whole cookin' thing, because after I made a post complaining about the obfuscated Lua, a very, very, very gifted person in Lua obfuscation and de-obfuscation contacted me and successfully deobfuscated it. I don't know if they want credit or not, because they're so cool and badass, but they're extremely famous in the Roblox hacking scene.  Anyway, the de-obfuscation is so precise it borders on having the actual source code to SmartLoader. I am very happy. I will share it when I am not dealing with my baby.

I'll share my super ghetto way of reverse engineering a Python2Exe thingy. You should not do it this way because it is extremely dangerous and you might accidentally shoot yourself in the foot. Only do it this super ghetto way if you're not a coward and not afraid of malware.  If you're afraid of malware, instead use Powershell and Strings64 to look for Python artefacts in the binary and name it like, malicious.bin, or something. You should also use a VM. I don't use a VM because I'm not a coward.  Step 1. Get a .exe Step 2. Is it Python2Exe? Step 3. Open in Notepad++ Step 4. Look for strings with Python3.dll (or similar) Step 5. Get pyinstxtractor from ExtremeCoders Step 6. Run pyinstxtractor against suspected malware Step 7. pyinstxtractor makes folder of suspected malware thingy like "\programthing.exe_extracted\" Step 8. Go inside folder Step 9. Look for programthingy.pyc Step 10. Throw in PyChaos or pylingual Step 11. ??? Step 12. Look at pictures of cats

Chat, someone sent me a message. It has a very silly payload.  I cannot figure out this Lua code. I hate obfuscated Lua. Look at this fucking piece of shit (warning: is a piece of shit).  gist.github.com/vxundergroun…
Cybersecurity researchers keep getting flagged and/or suspended on X.  Chat, it's only a matter of time for me. If I die, please collect pictures of cats in my honor.

Literally shaking, screaming, crying, throwing up right now. I thought I found a silly malware loader, but the person who wrote this bamboozled themselves.  Their obfuscation toolkit failed somewhere, and Windows WScript can't parse the VBS correctly, resulting in it imploding into itself.  It could have given me more free malware, instead this goof ball didn't test his malware (testing is for nerds, I can't blame them).  I think they may have copied the obfuscation definition thingies at the top too many times, but I don't feel like unironically debugging their obfuscated malicious VBS code to make it work, that is ridiculous.  Anyway, look at this piece of shit:  gist.github.com/vxundergroun…

I love when I meet nerds with who are deep in the trenches in esoteric concepts in esoteric things  I met a guy who has dedicated the past couple years of his life to Batch file obfuscation and deobfuscation  He has completely lost his mind. I like it. I respect it.

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-06-27 19:09 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

제공해주신 위협 인텔리전스 데이터를 분석한 결과입니다.

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 AI 코딩 도구(Amazon Q) 및 공급망(Supply Chain)의 취약점을 악용하여 클라우드 자격증명 탈취를 시도하는 등 공격 벡터를 지능화하고 있습니다. 특히 Signal Backup Recovery Keys 탈취와 같은 메신저 보안 우회 시도가 러시아 정보기관과 연계되어 나타나고 있으며, Cobalt Strike Beacon 배포를 위한 SharkLoader와 같은 신종 로더의 등장이 두드러집니다. 또한, AI 모델 및 에이전트 배포 환경에서의 인증 우회와 권한 상승 취약점이 다수 보고되고 있어, AI 기반 인프라의 보안 설정 강화가 시급한 상황입니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Russian Intelligence Services (FBI/CISA):** Signal 사용자 대상 피싱 및 Backup Recovery Keys 탈취 시도.
*   **Chinese-speaking APT (Unit42):** 동남아시아 정부 및 핵심 인프라 대상 TinyRCT 백도어 배포.
*   **Scattered Spider (Krebs on Security):** Transport for London 공격 관련 주요 인물 유죄 판결.
*   **Lazarus Group (Twitter/X):** Bybit 및 암호화폐 플랫폼 대상 대규모 자금 탈취 및 세탁 혐의.
*   **Gamaredon (WeLiveSecurity):** 터널링, Dead Drop, 신규 동맹을 활용한 C&C 인프라 은닉 및 데이터 탈취.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **Kimsuky:** 보안 담당자를 대상으로 개인정보 유출을 사칭한 스피어 피싱 공격 수행 ([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5765)).
*   **공급망 공격:** Chai.js 플러그인으로 위장한 북한발 악성 npm 패키지 'chai-as-init' 배포 ([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5766)).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-46331:** Linux Kernel (pedit COW), 로컬 권한 상승, 악용 중 ([TheHackerNews](https://twitter.com/TheHackersNews/status/2070528444568645906#m)).
*   **CVE-2026-12569:** PTC Windchill PDMlink, JSP web shell 드롭, 악용 중 ([TheHackerNews](https://twitter.com/TheHackersNews/status/2070492075024593004#m)).
*   **CVE-2026-12957/12958:** Amazon Q Developer, 클라우드 자격증명 탈취, 패치 완료 ([SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/amazon-q-flaw-enabled-cloud-credential-theft-via-malicious-repositories/)).
*   **CVE-2026-24207:** NVIDIA Triton Inference Server, 인증 우회, PoC 공개 ([DarkWebInformer](https://twitter.com/DarkWebInformer/status/2070663472476819624#m)).
*   **CVE-2026-3227:** TP-Link Routers, OS Command Injection, PoC 공개 ([DarkWebInformer](https://twitter.com/DarkWebInformer/status/2070671285990895695#m)).

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **도메인:** 없음
*   **해시:** 없음 (뉴스 본문에 파일 해시값 미포함)
*   **CVE:** CVE-2026-46331 ([TheHackerNews](https://twitter.com/TheHackersNews/status/2070528444568645906#m)), CVE-2026-12569 ([TheHackerNews](https://twitter.com/TheHackersNews/status/2070492075024593004#m)), CVE-2026-12957 ([SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/amazon-q-flaw-enabled-cloud-credential-theft-via-malicious-repositories/)), CVE-2026-24207 ([DarkWebInformer](https://twitter.com/DarkWebInformer/status/2070663472476819624#m)), CVE-2026-3227 ([DarkWebInformer](https://twitter.com/DarkWebInformer/status/2070671285990895695#m))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **Amazon Q 및 AI 도구 점검:** 개발 환경에서 사용 중인 AI 코딩 도구의 최신 패치를 적용하고, 외부 저장소(Repository) 로드 시 권한 격리 정책을 강화하십시오.
*   **Signal 보안 설정:** Signal 사용자에게 Backup Recovery Keys를 절대 외부로 공유하지 않도록 교육하고, 피싱 메일 주의를 당부하십시오.
*   **공급망 보안:** npm 패키지 등 오픈소스 라이브러리 사용 시 출처를 검증하고, 알려진 악성 패키지(chai-as-init 등)의 설치 여부를 전수 조사하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수:** 8/10
*   **근거:** 러시아 및 북한 연계 APT 그룹의 활동이 활발하며, AI 도구와 공급망을 악용한 고위험 취약점이 다수 발견되어 기업 인프라에 실질적인 위협이 되고 있음.
